OUESTIONS
1. If a group of four candidates recommend the candidates
and neasures to be endorsed in a slate mailer, is the slate rnailer
in question produced by a comrnercial rrslate rnailer organizationt
as defined in the Act?
2. If your organization does not quafify as a slate nailer
organization, how nust the joint-candidate mailer be financed in
order to avoid the making of contributions between the candidates?
CONCLUSIONS
1. A candidate or a candi,date's controlled conmittee cannot
becone a slate naj.ler organization. This is because the exception
provided for slate mailer organizations in the Act is based on the
non-political nature of the organization. Thus, if the four
candidates control who witl be included on the slate mailer and
how nuch each included candidate witl be charged, the sl'ate would
be produced by candidates and would not be a slate mailer produced
by a slate mailer organization.
2. If your organization does not qual.ify as a slate roailer
organization, al1 candidates who have been consulted and are
rrreconmendedrt on the slate must pay their pro-rata share of the
cost directly to the vendors. In addition, no candidate may pay
more than thE cost attributabLe to circulating the slate in his or
her o\.rn jurisdiction. 
only in this way is the rnakinq of a
contribution to another candidate avoided.
Candidates not consulted are not reguired to pay any part of
the cost of the slate. 
candidates who are so1e1y recommendors may
not pay any part of the cost if the connunication does not
exprlsily idvocate the reconmendor's election.
FACTS
You stated you are considering one or several mass mailings
which woul,d feature joint reconnendations by four candidates or
officeholders (the rriecorrmendorsrr) . One of the recommendors will

82013. If a slate nailer organization make's

Section 85304 Provides:
No candidate for elective office or committee
